extra.reasoning: focal_weakness appears only in r2 [priority 100] IF (sweating and rash and focal_weakness) THEN acuity := self_care, the highest-priority rule and the LOWEST acuity output. Take the baseline input sweating=true, rash=true, focal_weakness=false, all others false. With focal_weakness=false, r2 does not fire; the firing rules are r3 (sweating, priority 2 -> urgent) and r6 (rash, priority 3 -> emergent), so the priority-3 rule r6 wins and acuity = emergent (the highest level). Now set focal_weakness=true with everything else unchanged: r2 now fires at priority 100 and forces acuity = self_care, overriding r3 and r6. Acuity drops from emergent to self_care, the lowest level. Setting focal_weakness true thus lowers acuity, violating monotonicity. The witness assignment given is the focal_weakness=true (after) state that yields self_care; the comparison baseline is the identical assignment with focal_weakness=false, which yields emergent.
